rationale: Ground truth has zero substantive concerns (plain 'lgtm'), so both candidates trivially achieve full recall; the differentiator is precision/actionability of their own findings. Both independently verified the same upstream evidence (instrumentator/basic.py:23) confirming the rename fixes a real AttributeError bug, which is grounded and correct. X reaches the correct APPROVE verdict matching the actual review outcome, and explicitly recognizes the mock-test limitation as non-actionable rather than manufacturing a request. Y reaches COMMENT and asks for 'a targeted test that exercises create_speech, create_speech_batch, and create_audio_generate error paths end-to-end,' but the diff shows exactly these three paths already have updated regression tests, making that suggestion partly redundant/ungrounded and its actionability weaker (a vague either/or ask).
